elements.name: Hydrogen
elements.biologicalRole: Key component of water and all organic molecules
elements.didYouKnow: Hydrogen is the most abundant element in the universe.

elements.name: Helium
elements.didYouKnow: Helium is the second most abundant element in the universe.

elements.name: Lithium
elements.toxicity: Low toxicity; mood stabilizer in medicine
elements.didYouKnow: Lithium is the lightest metal and the least dense solid element.

elements.name: Beryllium
elements.didYouKnow: Beryllium is transparent to X-rays.

elements.name: Boron
elements.discoveredBy: Joseph Louis Gay-Lussac, Louis Jacques Thénard
elements.didYouKnow: Boron filaments are used in high-strength aerospace structures.

elements.name: Carbon
elements.didYouKnow: Carbon has the highest melting point of all elements.

elements.name: Nitrogen
elements.biologicalRole: Component of amino acids and DNA
elements.didYouKnow: Nitrogen makes up 78% of Earth's atmosphere.

elements.name: Oxygen
elements.didYouKnow: Liquid oxygen is pale blue and strongly paramagnetic.

elements.name: Fluorine
elements.didYouKnow: Fluorine is the most electronegative element.

elements.name: Neon
elements.appearance: Colorless gas; glows red-orange in discharge tube
elements.didYouKnow: Neon is the fourth most abundant element in the universe.

elements.name: Sodium
elements.toxicity: Non-toxic in compounds; reactive as metal
elements.biologicalRole: Essential for nerve function and fluid balance
elements.didYouKnow: Sodium is so soft it can be cut with a knife.

elements.name: Magnesium
elements.biologicalRole: Essential for chlorophyll and human enzymes
elements.didYouKnow: Magnesium burns with a brilliant white light.

elements.name: Aluminum
elements.didYouKnow: Aluminum is the most abundant metal in Earth's crust.

elements.name: Silicon
elements.biologicalRole: Essential for some plants and animals
elements.didYouKnow: Silicon is the second most abundant element in Earth's crust by mass.

elements.name: Phosphorus
elements.appearance: Colorless, waxy white, yellow, red, or black
elements.didYouKnow: Phosphorus was the first element discovered chemically.

elements.name: Sulfur
elements.biologicalRole: Found in amino acids cysteine and methionine
elements.appearance: Lemon yellow sintered cake or powder
elements.didYouKnow: Sulfur is known in the Bible as brimstone.

elements.name: Chlorine
elements.biologicalRole: Essential for fluid regulation in organisms
elements.didYouKnow: Chlorine was used as a chemical weapon in WWI.

elements.name: Argon
elements.didYouKnow: Argon is the most abundant noble gas in Earth's atmosphere.

elements.name: Potassium
elements.biologicalRole: Essential for nerve and muscle function
elements.didYouKnow: Potassium reacts violently with water.

elements.name: Calcium
elements.didYouKnow: Calcium is the fifth most abundant element in Earth’s crust.

elements.name: Scandium
elements.didYouKnow: Scandium is often stronger than aluminum when alloyed.

elements.name: Titanium
elements.didYouKnow: Titanium is as strong as steel but much lighter.

elements.name: Vanadium
elements.didYouKnow: Vanadium is found in some sea squirts as a blood pigment.

elements.name: Chromium
elements.didYouKnow: Chromium gives rubies their red color.

elements.name: Manganese
elements.didYouKnow: Manganese is important for photosynthesis in plants.

elements.name: Iron
elements.didYouKnow: Earth’s core is mostly iron.

elements.name: Cobalt
elements.didYouKnow: Cobalt blue glass was used in ancient Egypt.

elements.name: Nickel
elements.didYouKnow: Nickel is highly resistant to corrosion.

elements.name: Copper
elements.toxicity: Essential trace element; toxic in excess
elements.didYouKnow: Copper was one of the first metals used by humans.

elements.name: Zinc
elements.biologicalRole: Essential for immune system and enzymes
elements.didYouKnow: Zinc protects steel from rusting.

elements.name: Gallium
elements.didYouKnow: Gallium can melt in your hand.

elements.name: Germanium
elements.didYouKnow: Germanium was predicted before discovery by Mendeleev.

elements.name: Arsenic
elements.didYouKnow: Arsenic was once widely used as poison.

elements.name: Selenium
elements.toxicity: Essential in small amounts; toxic in excess
elements.didYouKnow: Selenium is important in thyroid function.

elements.name: Bromine
elements.didYouKnow: Bromine is one of only two liquid elements at room temperature.

elements.name: Krypton
elements.didYouKnow: Krypton was once used to define the meter.

elements.name: Rubidium
elements.didYouKnow: Rubidium can ignite spontaneously in air.

elements.name: Strontium
elements.didYouKnow: Strontium salts make fireworks bright red.

elements.name: Yttrium
elements.didYouKnow: Yttrium is used in red phosphors for TVs.

elements.name: Zirconium
elements.didYouKnow: Zirconium resists corrosion extremely well.

elements.name: Niobium
elements.didYouKnow: Niobium is used in superconducting magnets.

elements.name: Molybdenum
elements.didYouKnow: Molybdenum strengthens steel greatly.

elements.name: Technetium
elements.didYouKnow: Technetium was the first artificially produced element.

elements.name: Ruthenium
elements.didYouKnow: Ruthenium is used in electrical contacts.

elements.name: Rhodium
elements.didYouKnow: Rhodium is one of the rarest elements in Earth’s crust.

elements.name: Palladium
elements.didYouKnow: Palladium can absorb large amounts of hydrogen.

elements.name: Silver
elements.didYouKnow: Silver has the highest electrical conductivity of any metal.

elements.name: Cadmium
elements.didYouKnow: Cadmium is used in rechargeable batteries.

elements.name: Indium
elements.didYouKnow: Indium makes a crying sound when bent.

elements.name: Tin
elements.didYouKnow: Tin can transform into powder in extreme cold (tin pest).

elements.name: Antimony
elements.didYouKnow: Antimony expands slightly when it solidifies.

elements.name: Tellurium
elements.didYouKnow: Tellurium compounds can cause garlic-like breath odor.

elements.name: Iodine
elements.didYouKnow: Iodine vapor is violet in color.

elements.name: Xenon
elements.didYouKnow: Xenon is used in spacecraft ion engines.

elements.name: Cesium
elements.didYouKnow: Cesium is used in the definition of the second.

elements.name: Barium
elements.didYouKnow: Barium sulfate is used for medical imaging scans.

elements.name: Lanthanum
elements.didYouKnow: Lanthanum is used in hybrid car batteries.

elements.name: Cerium
elements.didYouKnow: Cerium is the most abundant rare earth element.

elements.name: Praseodymium
elements.didYouKnow: Praseodymium gives glass a yellow-green color.

elements.name: Neodymium
elements.didYouKnow: Neodymium magnets are among the strongest permanent magnets.

elements.name: Promethium
elements.discoveredBy: Jacob A. Marinsky, Lawrence E. Glendenin, Charles D. Coryell
elements.didYouKnow: Promethium is the only radioactive lanthanide with no stable isotopes.

elements.name: Samarium
elements.didYouKnow: Samarium-cobalt magnets work well at high temperatures.

elements.name: Europium
elements.didYouKnow: Europium gives red color in TV phosphors.

elements.name: Gadolinium
elements.didYouKnow: Gadolinium has exceptional neutron absorption ability.

elements.name: Terbium
elements.didYouKnow: Terbium is used in green phosphor displays.

elements.name: Dysprosium
elements.didYouKnow: Dysprosium improves heat resistance in magnets.

elements.name: Holmium
elements.didYouKnow: Holmium has the highest magnetic strength of any element.

elements.name: Erbium
elements.didYouKnow: Erbium is important in fiber optic communication amplifiers.

elements.name: Thulium
elements.didYouKnow: Thulium is the least abundant stable lanthanide.

elements.name: Ytterbium
elements.didYouKnow: Ytterbium is used in extremely precise optical clocks.

elements.name: Lutetium
elements.didYouKnow: Lutetium is one of the hardest and densest lanthanides.

elements.name: Hafnium
elements.didYouKnow: Hafnium absorbs neutrons extremely well.

elements.name: Tantalum
elements.didYouKnow: Tantalum is highly resistant to corrosion.

elements.name: Tungsten
elements.didYouKnow: Tungsten has the highest melting point of all pure metals.

elements.name: Rhenium
elements.discoveredBy: Walter Noddack, Ida Noddack, Otto Berg
elements.didYouKnow: Rhenium is one of the rarest elements in Earth’s crust.

elements.name: Osmium
elements.didYouKnow: Osmium is the densest naturally occurring element.

elements.name: Iridium
elements.didYouKnow: Iridium is associated with the dinosaur extinction asteroid layer.

elements.name: Platinum
elements.didYouKnow: Platinum is rarer than gold.

elements.name: Gold
elements.didYouKnow: Gold is so malleable that 1 gram can be beaten into a sheet of 1 square meter.

elements.name: Mercury
elements.didYouKnow: Mercury is the only metal liquid at room temperature.

elements.name: Thallium
elements.didYouKnow: Thallium was once used in rat poison.

elements.name: Lead
elements.didYouKnow: Lead was heavily used in ancient Roman plumbing.

elements.name: Bismuth
elements.didYouKnow: Bismuth crystals form beautiful rainbow oxide layers.

elements.name: Polonium
elements.didYouKnow: Polonium was named after Marie Curie’s homeland, Poland.

elements.name: Astatine
elements.discoveredBy: Dale R. Corson, Kenneth Ross MacKenzie, Emilio Segrè
elements.didYouKnow: Less than 1 gram of astatine exists naturally on Earth at any time.

elements.name: Radon
elements.didYouKnow: Radon exposure is a major cause of lung cancer after smoking.

elements.name: Francium
elements.didYouKnow: Francium is one of the rarest natural elements.

elements.name: Radium
elements.didYouKnow: Radium glows faintly in the dark due to radioactivity.

elements.name: Actinium
elements.didYouKnow: Actinium is over 150 times more radioactive than radium.

elements.name: Thorium
elements.didYouKnow: Thorium may be used as an alternative nuclear fuel to uranium.

elements.name: Protactinium
elements.didYouKnow: Protactinium is one of the rarest and most expensive naturally occurring elements.

elements.name: Uranium
elements.didYouKnow: Uranium powers nuclear reactors and atomic weapons.

elements.name: Neptunium
elements.didYouKnow: Neptunium was the first transuranium element discovered.

elements.name: Plutonium
elements.discoveredBy: Glenn T. Seaborg, Edwin McMillan and team
elements.didYouKnow: Plutonium-239 is widely used in nuclear weapons.

elements.name: Americium
elements.didYouKnow: Americium is commonly used in household smoke detectors.

elements.name: Curium
elements.discoveredBy: Glenn T. Seaborg, Ralph A. James, Albert Ghiorso
elements.didYouKnow: Curium is named after the Curies.

elements.name: Berkelium
elements.didYouKnow: Berkelium is mainly produced for scientific study.

elements.name: Californium
elements.discoveredBy: Stanley Thompson, Albert Ghiorso, Glenn T. Seaborg
elements.didYouKnow: Californium is a powerful neutron emitter.

elements.name: Einsteinium
elements.didYouKnow: Einsteinium was discovered in hydrogen bomb debris.

elements.name: Fermium
elements.didYouKnow: Fermium has never been produced in visible quantities.

elements.name: Fermium
elements.didYouKnow: Fermium has never been produced in visible quantities.

elements.name: Mendelevium
elements.discoveredBy: Albert Ghiorso, Glenn T. Seaborg and team
elements.didYouKnow: Mendelevium was named after the creator of the periodic table.

elements.name: Nobelium
elements.didYouKnow: Nobelium is named after Alfred Nobel, inventor of dynamite.

elements.name: Lawrencium
elements.discoveredBy: Albert Ghiorso, Torbjørn Sikkeland, Almon Larsh, Robert Latimer
elements.didYouKnow: Lawrencium is named after Ernest Lawrence, inventor of the cyclotron.

elements.name: Rutherfordium
elements.discoveredBy: Joint Institute for Nuclear Research / Lawrence Berkeley Laboratory
elements.didYouKnow: Rutherfordium is named after the father of nuclear physics.

elements.name: Dubnium
elements.didYouKnow: Dubnium was part of a major naming controversy.

elements.name: Seaborgium
elements.didYouKnow: Seaborgium is one of the few elements named after a living scientist.

elements.name: Bohrium
elements.didYouKnow: Bohrium is named after physicist Niels Bohr.

elements.name: Hassium
elements.didYouKnow: Hassium is named after the German state of Hesse.

elements.name: Meitnerium
elements.didYouKnow: Meitnerium honors physicist Lise Meitner.

elements.name: Darmstadtium
elements.didYouKnow: Darmstadtium was discovered in Darmstadt, Germany.

elements.name: Roentgenium
elements.didYouKnow: Roentgenium exists only for milliseconds in labs.

elements.name: Copernicium
elements.didYouKnow: Copernicium may behave more like a noble gas than a metal.

elements.name: Nihonium
elements.didYouKnow: Nihonium is the first element discovered in Asia.

elements.name: Flerovium
elements.didYouKnow: Flerovium may be unusually chemically inert.

elements.name: Moscovium
elements.didYouKnow: Moscovium atoms exist only briefly before decaying.

elements.name: Livermorium
elements.didYouKnow: Livermorium is named after a U.S. national laboratory.

elements.name: Tennessine
elements.didYouKnow: Tennessine is one of the newest officially named elements.

elements.name: Oganesson
elements.discoveredBy: Joint Institute for Nuclear Research + LLNL
elements.didYouKnow: Oganesson is the heaviest known element.
